commands/do.md.notes: New section '## Closing /do-developed work' (lines 8-26, 19 lines of content). Section is an AC-mandated procedural cross-reference with specific commands, specific paths, and concrete workflow steps. Matches 'rules not stories' pattern: tells users exactly what to do without philosophical narrative. Does not match any critical verbose pattern (Philosophy/Overview/Quality Standards/Safety Features/Helper Scripts). No finding.

commands/close.md.notes: Orphan commit 07d6e32 made 74-line changes (53 insertions, 41 deletions): (a) replaced inline bash blocks with prose equivalents (condensing, not expanding), (b) split the single-branch timestamp resolution into non-force/forced sub-branches to fix the qa-report inheritance bug, (c) renamed verdict branches 1b→2, 2→3, 3→4, 4→5, 5→6, 5b→7, 6→8, 7→9. Fix commit 400cd4b corrected 4 branch-number references that were still wrong after the orphan commit (verdict list 1/2/3/6/7/6→1/2/3/4/5/6/7, codex-disabled branch 7→9, em-dash annotation branch 7→9, cross-ref branch 2→3). All new content is procedural decision trees, numbered branch definitions, and spec cross-references — not Philosophy/Overview/Quality Standards/Safety Features/Helper Scripts. The section '## Constraints' and the QA dispatch prompt block are not new sections introduced in this diff; they were pre-existing content modified in-place (branch-number corrections). No verbose pattern section was introduced.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 4 OBSERVATION_ONLY items. None carried a PROPOSED_FIX. (1) No missed verbosity violation: commands/do.md new section is short, AC-required, and procedural; must not be counted as verbose even though it is a large percentage of the small file. (2) No false positives: draft reports no findings, no overcounting. (3) No severity misclassification: there is no qualifying verbose_lines section to threshold. (4) Draft summary in the original analysis overstates some commands/close.md additions as 'newly added branches/plumbing' when much was pre-existing content only renumbered/reworded — this is an observation, not a blocker, because the no-finding conclusion remains correct.

findings.issue: No missed verbosity violation found. commands/do.md new section is short, AC-required, and procedural; should not be counted as verbose even though it is a large percentage of the small file.
findings.rationale: Codex confirms the no-finding verdict for do.md. The section is 19 lines of procedural instruction, not a Philosophy/Overview/Quality Standards section.

findings.issue: No false positives in the draft because it reports no findings.
No severity misclassification found; there is no qualifying verbose_lines section to threshold.
findings.rationale: Affirms that thresholds (critical>=200, major>=100, minor>=50) are correctly not triggered because verbose_lines=0.

findings.issue: Draft summary overstates some commands/close.md additions as newly added branches/plumbing. Much of that content was pre-existing and only renumbered/reworded. This is not a blocker because the no-finding conclusion remains correct.
findings.rationale: Accurate observation. The analysis notes in the final report have been updated to reflect that the close.md changes are primarily renumbering of pre-existing verdict branches with targeted wording corrections, not wholesale new content. No-finding conclusion unchanged.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 codex findings were OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX. No changes to the no-finding verdict. Analysis notes updated to reflect that close.md changes are primarily renumbering of pre-existing verdict branches rather than wholly new content.
